title: Neurodivergence, Family and Faith
author: Kate Morris and David Whittingham
description: Neurodivergence, Family and Faith is a podcast for Christian parents raising neurodivergent kids, and for the churches and friends who walk alongside them. Each episode offers practical tools, grounded in research and shaped by the wisdom of the Bible. Hosts Kate Morris and Dave Whittingham draw on their backgrounds in theology, education, and ministry, as well as the experiences of their families. Both are parents of neurodivergent kids, and Dave is neurodivergent himself. Kate has completed further study in neurodivergence, and is an Honorary Research Fellow with Mary Andrews College (Australian University of Theology). Together, they bring both understanding and expertise as they explore topics like managing energy, navigating sensory differences, and finding joy and belonging in church and family life. Whether you’re doing the dishes, chasing the kids, or catching a quiet moment, this podcast offers comfort, encouragement, and guidance to help your family thrive for God’s glory.

title: Listen-Think-Live
author: Northumbrian Collective
description:
Come and join Roy, Helen, Craig, Julie and Simon who together form the Northumbrian Collective. We are united in a shared commitment to Listen Well, Think Deeply and Live Authentically in a challenging world.
As friends from different backgrounds and life experiences, and with different gifts and ministries, we believe it is possible to build bridges of understanding, uncover ancient wisdom for modern times and form resilient communities of hope. As well as sharing Christian faith, we appreciate the gift of friendship, honest conversations, good food and wine, and laughter.

title: Sandhills Community Church Sermons

description: Weekly sermons from Sunday morning services at Sandhills Community Church in Columbia, SC.

speaker_display: Pastor Paul Amlin Lutheran Church of the Resurrection Cocoa Beach, FL
description: Weekly messages about life, faith, and the connection between them.

title: Preaching Well
author: Rt Rev Saju Varghese Muthalaly, Diocese of Leicester
speaker_display: Rt Rev Saju Varghese Muthalaly, Diocese of Leicester
description:
Preaching Well is where the art of preaching meets the pulse of the world. Hosted by Bishop Saju Muthalaly, Bishop of Loughborough in the Diocese of Leicester, this podcast explores how Christians can speak Scripture with power and precision—across cultures, contexts, and generations.
You’ll hear from world-class voices:
Rowan Williams, Kelly Brown Douglas, Martyn Snow, Stephen Cottrell, Mark Oakley, Guli Francis-Dehqani, Jared Alcántara and more - bringing wisdom, wit, and wonder.
Wherever you preach, this is your space to grow as a preacher who speaks with clarity, compassion, and courage.

title: Stunned by Scripture
author: St. Paul Center for Biblical Theology
speaker_display: St. Paul Center for Biblical Theology
description: Renowned Scripture scholar Dr. John Bergsma teams up with special guests to unpack the rich mysteries of Sacred Scripture. In Stunned by Scripture—a bi-weekly podcast from the St. Paul Center—Dr. Bergsma and his expert guests tackle the challenging questions of biblical interpretation with clarity, passion, and a healthy dose of Catholic wit. From the historical and cultural background of the Old Testament to biblical apologetics to practical biblical wisdom for everyday living, Stunned by Scripture will bring Scripture to life like never before. So grab your Bible and let’s get started.

title: The Homily
author: St. Paul San Antonio
description: On Sunday mornings, we are encouraged by the Word & empowered by the Sacraments. However, we all know that the week gets hard. That is why this podcast aims to give you a glimpse of hope in the midst of the workday, while the kids take a nap, or during your morning run. Whatever your season of life is, the truth, goodness, & beauty of the gospel will seek you relentlessly.

title: KLC Preaching Podcast
author: The Kyle Lake Center for Effective Preaching
speaker_display: The Kyle Lake Center for Effective Preaching
description: The mission of the Kyle Lake Center for Effective Preaching is to equip preachers at every level to engage a diverse world for the sake of the gospel. Our vision is to empower preachers to engage in faithful and fitting proclamation of the gospel by providing resources, training, and learning opportunities. The Center is housed within Baylor University's George W. Truett Theological Seminary. It was established in 2006 by David and Shirley Lake in memory of their son Kyle.

title: Layman’s Homily
author: laymanshomily
description: Two brothers, Tony and Brandon D’Arienzo, both devout Catholics and engineers from the Georgia Institute of Technology, invite you into to join their podcast as they address questions stemming from living out our Catholic faith in the modern age. Our mission statement: To leverage the power of mass communication to connect with other Catholics striving for holiness in their daily lives. We started The Layman’s Homily because we are both passionate about our faith, and we both enjoy reading and writing. We hope, by sharing our experiences, that you will be empowered to continue fighting and striving to be a Catholic man or woman, because it is worth fighting for.

title: BFF OT Bible Teaching
author: Paul J. Bucknell
description: Biblical Foundations for Freedom presents Old Testament Bible teaching by Paul J. Bucknell. This audio feed gathers messages from across the Old Testament.

title: PREACH FREEDOM
author: Alex Umole
description: Preach Freedom is a conversation starter featuring ordinary people such as laity, pastors, and professionals in secular workplaces having honest and unfiltered conversations about social justice and the church's role. The ideas and perspectives discussed are then contextualized to South Florida to seek ways local Pentecostal churches can embrace new strategies in social justice to impact the lives of people in society beyond the four walls of the church. Join the Conversation. Write to aumole18@georgefox.edu.

title: Dating and Desire Podcast
author: Bridgett Blood and Lauren Mathues
description: The Dating & Desire Podcast is a Christian dating and sexuality podcast for singles and dating adults who want more than rules or fear-based answers. Hosted by relationship coaches Bridgett Blood and Lauren Mathues, the show explores Christian dating, sexual desire, emotional safety, boundaries, and integrity. Through honest conversations with pastors, therapists, and relationship experts, we help you navigate physical boundaries and sexual formation with faith and self-trust. Because your desire is holy, your body is good, and your story matters.

title: Commentary on the Gospel of Luke, Sermons 126-140
author: Cyril of Alexandria
description: Dive into an insightful exploration of the Gospel According to Luke, focusing on Sermons 126 through 140, which cover passages from Luke 1835 to 226. Join The Reader as they provide a thoughtful summary and analysis of these profound teachings.

title: Joseph Prince Audio Podcast
author: Joseph Prince
description:
With more than two decades of full-time ministry behind him, Joseph Prince is today a leading voice in proclaiming the gospel of grace around the world through his books, teaching resources, and television ministry. 

The author of best sellers such as The Power of Right Believing, Destined To Reign, and Unmerited Favor, Joseph is also a highly sought-after conference speaker. He has impacted church leaders worldwide by preaching the unadulterated gospel of Jesus with boldness. He is known for teaching God's Word in a fresh, practical, and revelatory way that always unveils Jesus. His humorous, dynamic, and engaging style of preaching has also endeared him to a wide spectrum of viewers who tune in to his daily television program. His broadcast currently reaches millions of homes across North America, Europe, Africa, Australia, and Israel on both secular and Christian networks. 

A founding member of New Creation Church, Joseph initially served as an elder and associate pastor. However, his unanimous appointment as senior pastor in 1990 marked a turning point in the history of the church, which started experiencing phenomenal growth. Under Joseph's leadership, the church congregatio

title: Commentary on the Gospel of Luke, Sermons 66-80 by Cyril of Alexandria (c. 376 - 444)
author: LibriVox
description: Sermons 66 through 80 cover the Gospel According to Luke 10:22 to 11:18. - Summary by the Reader

title: The Faith Filled Therapy Podcast
author: The Faith Filled Therapist
description:
Hello to you, I’m glad you’re here. 


The Faith Filled Therapy podcast sits at the intersection of Scripture, neuroscience, and therapeutic insight, exploring how we actually experience change, not just talk about it.


Each episode gently unpacks what it means to renew your mind in a real, lived way,  looking at thought patterns, the nervous system, and the inner world we carry, all through a lens that holds neuroscience with curiosity, while ultimately submitting it to the truth of Scripture.


This isn’t about quick fixes or surface-level faith. It’s about understanding why you feel the way you feel, learning how your mind and body work, and discovering how transformation happens-spirit, soul and synapses.


If you’ve ever loved God but still felt stuck in your thoughts, overwhelmed in your emotions, or unsure how faith connects to your mental health, you’re not alone.


This podcast will help you make sense of your inner world, and take small, meaningful steps towards wholeness.

title: Questioning Christianity with Tim Keller
author: Tim Keller | Gospel in Life
description:
Questioning Christianity with Tim Keller is a seven-part podcast series for people who are interested in exploring Christianity, brought to you by Gospel in Life. This series will help listeners work through tough questions like: Can there be moral absolutes? Does life have meaning beyond what I make of it? Can hope exist in the face of death?

Each of the talks and Q&A sessions were given by Tim Keller in 2019 before a live gathering in New York City, made up primarily of attendees who did not identify as Christian.

title: Chuck Swindoll Sermons on Lightsource.com - Audio
author: Chuck Swindoll
description:
Pastor Chuck Swindoll pastors at Stonebriar Community Church in Frisco, TX.  His sermons are available here in their entirety. Watch the live service, with Chuck preaching, at 10:40 a.m. Central time each Sunday.

To support this ministry financially, visit: https://www.lightsource.com/donate/1447

description: These Sunday Sermon messages form a collection of the most effective and fruitful sermons given by Dr. J. Vernon McGee during his 21-year pastorate (1949-1970) at the historic Church of the Open Door when it was located in downtown Los Angeles.Other Thru the Bible Programs:Thru the BibleThru the Bible - Minute with McGeeThru the Bible - Questions  AnswersThru the Bible InternationalA Través de la Biblia

author: Justin Hoff
description:
Welcome to Justin's In Your Corner Bible Teaching, where Scripture interprets Scripture, and the Holy Spirit with God's living Word will do the correcting, healing, comforting, and preparing of hearts. I truly look forward to sharing these messages, and God willing, these messages bless you, your family, and friends.


If you would like to be a guest on the podcast, simply DM me on any of the platforms herein. During our studies, I will share many Biblical resources from which will help equip and deepen our faith journey in drawing closer to Jesus, navigate our world and prepare for End Times.


#podcast #fyp #shorts #Jesus #Yeshua #JustinsInYourCornerBibleTeaching #biblestudy

description: The podcast where we cut through the noise of culture to help teen girls and young women build a confident, Christian worldview.

title: Starting Strong: Thriving in the First Years of Preaching
author: Matt & Oli
description:
Preaching is hard, especially in your first years. Starting Strong: Thriving in the First Years of Preaching brings you wisdom from experienced preachers to help you stay faithful, encouraged, and growing. We’re not the experts but we’re bringing them to you, with real conversations, practical tips, and hard-hitting truths to sharpen and sustain you for the road ahead.

Start strong. Preach well. Stay true.

Whether you are a Christian or are a seeker exploring the depths of spirituality, our podcast offers insightful sermons and reflections to inspire, uplift, and comfort your soul.

title: The Learning to Preach Podcast
author: Coram Deo Church
description: Preaching is a craft; and like any craft, it can be learned. The Learning to Preach podcast helps preachers of the Bible learn the tools of the trade, identify key mistakes, and develop skill in preparing and delivering gospel-centered, Christ-exalting sermons.

title: Dormition Parish Homily Replay
author: Dormition Parish Saskatoon
description: The Sunday homily doesn’t have to end at the church doors. You can now listen to the weekly message anytime and carry that reflection with you throughout the week.

title: Sinclair Ferguson Sermons
author: Expository Gospel-Centred Sermons
description: Messages delivered by Dr. Sinclair Ferguson. These sermons are not meant to take the place of worshipping and belonging to a local church (Heb 10:23-25). // This podcast is not affiliated with Dr. Sinclair Ferguson or his ministry.

title: Prepare the Way Podcast With Nate & Christy
author: Nate & Christy Johnston
description:
Prepare the Way is a Christ-centered, solutions-focused podcast for those who refuse to sit on the sidelines. In the midst of growing darkness in the world, the church, and society, Nate and Christy dive into real and raw issues, what’s happening in the news, culture, and the Body of Christ, and speak into them with prophetic insight, the Word of the Lord, and spiritual strategy.

Each episode is a call to intercession and action. This is not just about commentary, it’s about spiritual warfare, breaking things down, confronting deception, and releasing heaven’s perspective. It’s about preparing a pure Bride who understands the times and is ready to stand, pray, and occupy until He comes.

Prepare the Way is for those who are done with passive Christianity and ready to contend for truth, walk in discernment, and partner with God’s redemptive purposes in the earth.

title: "The Gospel of Grace" A Sermon Series through the book of Galatians
author: Renewed Life Church
description:
The book of Galatians contends for the clear & simple Gospel of Grace with greater clarity and strength than any other Biblical document. Martin Luther famously referred to it as his wife, because of how it captivated his own heart - and led to the Great Reformation of the Christian Church.
Join us, as we step in this amazing book together.

This sermon series was preached in 2023.
*Our church changed its name as we stepped into 2025 from Olive Branch Christian Fellowship, to Renewed Life Church. Renewed Life is a part of the Calvary Global Network (CGN) of churches. Visit us at RLCsd.org

Join us as Alistair helps us apply the Scripture to our daily lives.

Each message focuses on following Jesus, growing in faith, and sharing hope with others through practical application of Scripture. Whether you are part of a house church, faith community, or listening on your own, our prayer is that these teachings strengthen your walk with Jesus and equip you to live as His witness in everyday life.

title: The Forming Men Podcast
author: Jefferson Bethke & Jon Tyson
description: Join Jefferson Bethke & Jon Tyson as they discuss being a man in the modern world--all it's complications, wrestlings, and challenges--while pointing to Jesus and what he can teach us to reshape us as men in his own image.

title: Two Jesuits Talking
author: Jesuit Media Lab (Society of Jesus)
description:
Two Jesuits Talking is a Catholic podcast where Fr. Eric Immel, SJ, and Fr. Damian Torres-Botello, SJ share real stories of ministry, friendship, doubt, joy, and the unexpected ways God shows up. These are honest, unscripted conversations rooted in Ignatian spirituality, where laughter and depth go hand in hand. Not a lecture, just two Jesuits making sense of life and faith together. Have a listen!

TJT is a production of Jesuit Media Lab, from the Jesuit Conference of Canada and the United States. To learn more about JML’s events, resources, and other podcasts, visit jesuitmedialab.org.

title: Jesus and Jewish Law
author: Logan Williams and Paul Sloan
description: Two New Testament scholars who specialise in Jewish law explore how Jesus interprets and applies the Mosaic law in the canonical gospels.

author: Loyal Books

title: Insight with Karl Faase
author: Olive Tree Media
description: Hosted by Karl Faase, Insight delves into the big themes of life, faith, and culture through the power of story. Each conversation opens a window into the experiences of everyday people, leaders, thinkers, and creatives who have navigated life’s challenges and found hope along the way. Rooted in a Christian worldview but open to all, Insight aims to spark thoughtful reflection and encourage deeper engagement with the world around us.

title: The Sermon On The Mount
author: Pastor Jeremy Higgins
description: This teaching series through the Sermon On The Mount was taught by Pastor Jeremy Higgins at Calvary Community Church in Cedar Rapids, Iowa.  For more information about Pastor Jeremy, or our Church, visit us online at www.CalvaryCedarRapids.com.

title: Preaching In Youth Ministry
author: Preaching in Youth Ministry
description:
Welcome to The Preaching In Youth Ministry Podcast. This is a podcast dedicated to helping youth pastors be faithful preachers. Join us as we cover everything from theology of preaching, developing a sermon calendar, preaching through books of the Bible, and so much more. 

Please send questions, comments, and requests for specific episodes to preachinginyouthministry@gmail.com. 

Thanks for listening. Please subscribe, review, and share with any youth pastor you know!

title: SUNDAY READINGS AND HOMILY
author: ST. ANTHONY CATHOLIC CHURCH NUNGUA
description: Sunday liturgical readings and homilies from St. Anthony Catholic Church, Nungua

title: Gospel Outreach: The Sermons of Tom Peterson
author: Tom Peterson
description: Tom Peterson preached powerful sermons through the 80's and the 2000's from the Bible. Now these awesome messages are being shared with everyone!

title: Expository Preaching: Voices in the Wilderness
author: Antonio Tremayne Dixon, Sr.
description: "Expository Preaching: Voices in the Wilderness" is a podcast focused on equipping preachers, pastors, and those seeking to engage in pastoral ministry with helpful tools and insights regarding expository preaching. While this is the targeted audience, the information shared through these episodes will be beneficial to all who listen. Make sure to subscribe to the available platforms. New episodes will be released every month.

title: Bible Exposition With Lo
author: Bible Exposition With Lo
description:
This is a podcast for every Christian particularly those that are willing to learn and be exposed to the limitless riches of the word.
With the help and guidance of the Holy Spirit, myself and some friends (from time to time) will be explaining scriptures and also address vital issues with respect to our Christian journey.
 Contributions,questions and reviews are welcome. Connect Now!
Email: Oluwayomide@gmail.com

title: The Preaching Lab
author: Clif Johnson
description: Welcome to The Preaching Lab Podcast, where we believe healthy pastors make healthy pulpits. Each episode is designed to encourage pastors toward spiritual health, personal holiness, and faithful exposition of God’s Word. Hosted by Adam Thomas and Jason Goodwin, Baptist pastors and co-leaders of The Preaching Lab Ministry, this podcast offers honest conversations, biblical encouragement, and practical wisdom for those called to preach. Join us as we talk about the soul of the preacher, the craft of expository preaching, and the rhythms that sustain long-term faithfulness in ministry. Whether you’re a seasoned pastor, a student of preaching, or someone longing to grow in biblical exposition, The Preaching Lab Podcast exists to help you stay healthy — in heart, in mind, and in the pulpit.

title: Clear Preaching
author: Dr Jonathan McClintock
description:
You worked hard on that sermon. Did they actually hear it?


Clear Preaching is the podcast for preachers who are serious about closing the gap between what they meant to say and what their congregation actually heard. Hosted by Dr. Jonathan McClintock — preacher, pastor, sixteen-year homiletics instructor, and developer of the four-domain Clear Preaching Framework — each episode delivers practical, framework-driven teaching on the discipline of preaching with clarity.


Through solo teaching episodes, conversations with preachers and scholars, and real sermon analysis, Clear Preaching helps you develop clarity at every stage of the preaching process — from the moment you open the text in your study to the moment you close your Bible in the pulpit.


Whether you are stepping into the pulpit for the first time or have been preaching for decades — if you believe the message you carry is worth delivering as clearly as possible, this podcast is for you.

title: Classic Holiness Sermons
author: Dylan Damrell
description: A Podcast of the great Pentecostal Holiness Preachers in the movement. We upload sermons from the great men of God that has blazed the trail for many generations to come. We are attempting to persevere these classic sermons for those that cherish these life changing sermons. Thank you for listening!!

title: Father Hudgins' Homilies
author: Father James Hudgins
description: Homilies given by Father James Hudgins, Pastor of St. Theresa Catholic Church in Ashburn, Virginia. The seven most recent homilies are available via podcast; for the archives, visit www.FatherHudgins.com.

title: Live No Lies Podcast
author: John Mark Comer
description: How do we follow Jesus in a cultural moment that is increasingly hostile to Christian orthodoxy of any kind? Join John Mark Comer as he interviews leading Christian intellectuals about the intersection of post-Christian culture and spiritual formation. If you want to take the conversation deeper, order John Mark’s new book "Live No Lies: Recognize and Resist the Three Enemies that Sabotage Your Peace" today. Available wherever books are sold.

title: Studies in the Sermon on the Mount
author: Oswald Chambers
description: In this insightful work, Oswald Chambers delves into the depths of Jesus’ most famous teachings, offering profound reflections and interpretations that illuminate the timeless wisdom of the Sermon on the Mount. (Summary by progressingamerica)

title: Expository Preaching for Impact
author: Tony Guthrie, PHD.
description: Tony Guthrie from Learning and Developing Expository Preaching reveals all of his expository sermon preparation and delivery principles so you can be ahead of the preaching curve and make meaningful biblical impact on your listeners and see them transformed for God's glory. Discover how you can interpret scripture and develop expository messages that cause your congregants to develop a deeper appreciation for God's Word and for living a meaningful and impactful Christian life. Dr. Guthrie will help you develop outlines that work, understand principles of interpretation even better, connect with your listeners with meaningful application, and take your preaching to a higher level. Hit subscribe and get ready to change your life!

title: Praying Like Monks, Living Like Fools Podcast
author: Tyler Staton
description: Praying Like Monks, Living Like Fools is a one season, six episode podcast from Tyler Staton. Each episode Tyler has conversation with friends and thought leaders on all things prayer, including silence and solitude, adoration, intercession, and even unanswered prayer. Join us every Monday starting at the end of October for new episodes featuring special guests John Mark Comer, Christine Caine, Pete Greig, Tim Mackie, and JT Thomas.

title: Vanquish Darkness with Andrew and Kelly Whalen
author: Andrew and Kelly Whalen
description:
An unfiltered view of our world in the context of spiritual warfare, through the lens of Christ's victory in order that we not only see darkness, but are equipped to overcome and vanquish it! 

Episodes touch on relevant topics for the 'ekklesia' effective for waging a good warfare, and advancing Christ's Kingdom. 

Vanquish Darkness provides viewers with prophetic insight that draws from dreams, encounters, biblical truth, and personal experience.

title: Bayou Baptist Pulpit Preaching
author: Bayou Drive Baptist Church
description: A friendly church just 30 minutes south of Houston's downtown emphasizing text-driven Bible preaching.

title: EASTER SERMONS
author: Pastor Robert Griffith
description: Easter Sermons by Pastor Robert Griffith

title: Preaching Magazine Podcast
author: Preaching Magazine
description: The Preaching Magazine Podcast is where preaching and practice meet. Each episode features conversations with pastors, scholars, and ministry leaders who share insights on the craft of preaching, the challenges facing today’s church, and the timeless power of God’s Word. Whether you’re stepping into the pulpit for the first time or have decades of ministry experience, this podcast will equip, encourage, and inspire you to preach with clarity, conviction, and creativity.

title: Her Work: Reframed by Jesus
author: City Bible Forum
description: A podcast exploring what it means to work well in God’s world as we seek to serve God and others. Her Work is primarily for women, but it will resonate with anyone thinking seriously about faith and work.

title: Better Preaching Podcast
author: Dr. Frank Weller
description: Powered by Great Lakes Christian College, the Better Preaching Podcast (BP2) is inspiring a new generation of preachers to embrace the high calling and challenges of writing and delivering biblical, life-changing sermons.

title: GCC Sermon Podcast
author: Gospel Community Church
description: Podcast by Gospel Community Church

title: Jesus Is Lord (live Preaching)
author: Jesus Is Lord Church
description: Faith Comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God. Romans 10:17

title: Telugu Christian Sermons
author: John Vunnamatla
description: A Christian Sermon Podcast for Telugu speaking people by Pastor John Vunnamatla. Pas. John is the pastor of Indian Baptist Church. He is the son of a renowned preacher in the 90's, Rev. Dr. V. Daivabhimani. Pastor John graduated with a Bachelors and a Masters degree from a renowned institution in Phoenix, AZ. He now serves with his wife Emily in Andhra Pradesh, India. They have a beautiful daughter Alyssa.

title: Preaching While Queer
author: Joshua Clapp
description:
Creating Space for Queer people in ministry to share their story, connect with others, and find support. 

About the host - Joshua Clapp

Josh (He/Him/His) is a student at University of Dubuque Theological Seminary seeking his Master of Divinity and pursuing ordination in the Presbyterian Church USA. 
Josh is a trans man and strong advocate for the queer community, particularly in ministry settings. He is passionate lifting voices of the oppressed creating safe spaces and has dreams of writing a book and speaking about his experience as a queer person of faith.

title: Just Homilies
author: Just Homilies
description: Mini sermons for the disillusioned, the deconstructed, the tired-of-church, or those who’ve never stepped inside one, but still feel drawn to explore Christian spirituality through God’s Word. No matter how you arrive, this space is for you.

description: Podcast by Ken Campbell

description: Podcast by Parkside Gospel Church

title: Sermons Out of Rural Ministry
author: Pastor Josh Ehrler
description: Dynamic, contextual sermons from Pastor Josh Ehrler, who serves a small town ELCA Lutheran congregation in Northern Illinois. Sermons are rooted in scripture and reflect the local community, as well as the movements of the wider world. He and his congregation are affirming of LGBTQ+ personhood and live out their baptismal promise to seek (racial, economic, identity, environmental) justice and peace.

description: Welcome to HOELY HOMILY , a podcast where we freely discuss about everything life , fun, facts and of course cruise.

description: Christian talks, sermons and teaching from a weekly radio program in Melbourne, Australia called "Songs of Hope". The talks are sourced from local pastors in Melbourne. Talk time varies from 5 minutes to 25 minutes.
